content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 8, 2011


Anthony Lewis
General Counsel
California Technology Agency
1325 J Street, Suite 1600
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:    Your Request for Informal Assistance
       Our File No. I-11-072

Dear Mr. Lewis:

       This letter responds to your request for advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your question is general in nature, we are treating
your request as one for informal assistance.²

       You are the General Counsel for the California Technology Agency ("CTA") and are
writing on behalf of Lee Mosbrucker, CTA's Deputy Director of Enterprise Architecture. Lee
Mosbrucker has submitted a Form 700 that identifies investments with technology companies
conducting business with the CTA. Mr. Mosbrucker's investment in each company is worth
$2,000 or more. The companies include Cisco Systems and Impression Technology, Inc. You
have not identified any governmental decisions that are currently before or will be before
Mr. Mosbrucker at this time, though you state that he may be required to provide technical
guidance to agencies, review policies, and advise the Agency on the development and
maintenance of statewide technology systems that are provided by or supported by companies in
which Mr. Mosbrucker has an interest as described above.

       You wish to know whether Mr. Mosbrucker's investments in the above named companies
would pose a conflict-of-interest with his duties as Deputy Director of Enterprise Architecture.


¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


---~

File No. I-11-072
Page No. 2


Please note that determining whether a conflict-of-interest exists in a given situation is
necessarily a fact-sensitive analysis.

Because your inquiry is general in nature and does not involve specific governmental
decisions, we have enclosed the following advice letters for your review: Larson Advice Letter,
I-06-073 and Reyes Advice Letter, A-04-210 along with our pamphlet "Can I Vote." We hope
that this information will be helpful in providing general guidance with regard to
Mr. Mosbrucker's situation.

In the future, should you have questions regarding a specific governmental decision,
please contact us for further advice.

Sincerely,

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

[signature]

By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division

SKB:jgl

Enclosures

sections.question: Whether Mr. Mosbrucker's investments in technology companies (Cisco Systems and Impression Technology, Inc.), with which the California Technology Agency conducts business, would pose a conflict-of-interest with his duties as Deputy Director of Enterprise Architecture.
sections.conclusion: The FPPC did not provide a definitive conclusion, noting that determining whether a conflict-of-interest exists is necessarily a fact-sensitive analysis. Instead, the FPPC provided reference materials (two prior advice letters and a pamphlet) and advised that if there are questions regarding a specific governmental decision, the requestor should contact the FPPC for further advice.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a public official's personal investments in technology companies that conduct business with their employer create a conflict-of-interest when the official may provide technical guidance and policy advice affecting those companies' contracts?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to issue formal advice due to the general nature of the inquiry and lack of specific governmental decisions, instead directing the requestor to reference materials and encouraging contact regarding specific decisions in the future.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal Q/A sections present; informal advice letter. The letter discusses the factual scenario but does not provide a specific conclusion or binding advice. References to enclosed advice letters (Larson I-06-073 and Reyes A-04-210) provided as guidance. Document treated as informal assistance, not formal opinion, and therefore does not provide immunity under Section 83114.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Mr. Mosbrucker's investments in technology companies (Cisco Systems and Impression Technology, Inc.), with which the California Technology Agency conducts business, would pose a conflict-of-interest with his duties as Deputy Director of Enterprise Architecture.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC did not provide a definitive conclusion, noting that determining whether a conflict-of-interest exists is necessarily a fact-sensitive analysis. Instead, the FPPC provided reference materials (two prior advice letters and a pamphlet) and advised that if there are questions regarding a specific governmental decision, the requestor should contact the FPPC for further advice.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a request for informal advice regarding whether a CTA Deputy Director's investments in technology companies doing business with the agency constitute a conflict-of-interest. The FPPC treats this as informal assistance and declines to provide definitive guidance, instead referring the requestor to prior advice letters and materials.
